de.uka.ilkd.key.control

Class DefaultUserInterfaceControl

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.control.AbstractUserInterfaceControl
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.control.DefaultUserInterfaceControl
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    UserInterfaceControl, ProblemInitializer.ProblemInitializerListener, ProblemLoaderControl, ProverTaskListener, ProgressMonitor

    public class DefaultUserInterfaceControl
extends AbstractUserInterfaceControl
    The DefaultUserInterfaceControl which allows proving in case that no specific user interface is available.

    In case that no user interface should be used see also KeYEnvironment which provides static methods to load source code and to instantiate this class.

    Author:
    Martin Hentschel
    See Also:
    KeYEnvironment

    • Method Detail

      • getProofControl

        public DefaultProofControl getProofControl()
        Returns the used ProofControl.
        Returns:
        The used ProofControl.

      • createProofEnvironmentAndRegisterProof

        public ProofEnvironment createProofEnvironmentAndRegisterProof(ProofOblInput proofOblInput,
                                                      ProofAggregate proofList,
                                                      InitConfig initConfig)
        registers the proof aggregate at the UI
        Specified by:
        createProofEnvironmentAndRegisterProof in class AbstractUserInterfaceControl
        Parameters:
        proofOblInput - the ProofOblInput
        proofList - the ProofAggregate
        initConfig - the InitConfig to be used
        Returns:
        the new ProofEnvironment where the ProofAggregate has been registered

      • selectProofObligation

        public boolean selectProofObligation(InitConfig initConfig)
        This method is called if no IPersistablePO.LoadedPOContainer was created via #createProofObligationContainer() and can be overwritten for instance to open the proof management dialog as done by ProblemLoader.
        Returns:
        true if the proof obligation was selected, and false if action was aborted

      • setProgress

        public void setProgress(int progress)
        Set the progress achieved so far. The parameter progress has to be >=0 and <= to the maximum value previously set with ProgressMonitor.setMaximum(int).
        Parameters:
        progress - number of steps completed

      • setMaximum

        public void setMaximum(int maximum)
        Set the maximum number of steps for this task.
        Parameters:
        maximum - maximum number of steps, >=0.

      • registerProofAggregate

        public void registerProofAggregate(ProofAggregate pa)
        Registers an already created ProofAggregate in this UserInterfaceControl.
        Parameters:
        pa - The ProofAggregate to register.
